name: PDE5 class-transfer and agent-directness framework targeted baseline search

search_queries.query: CIALIS VIAGRA vardenafil STENDRA prescribing information half-life PDE5 PDE6 PDE11 nitrates GC stimulators
phosphodiesterase type 5 inhibitor differentiation selectivity pharmacokinetic efficacy profiles sildenafil tadalafil vardenafil
potency selectivity consequences nonselectivity PDE inhibition tadalafil sildenafil vardenafil PDE6 PDE11
PDE5 inhibitors LiverTox sildenafil tadalafil vardenafil avanafil nitric oxide cGMP
PDE5 inhibitors pharmacology clinical applications half-life sildenafil tadalafil vardenafil avanafil review
search_queries.result_count: screened for newer open review context; labels and NCBI sources prioritized

inclusion_criteria: Current or latest accessible official U.S. labels for tadalafil, sildenafil, vardenafil, and avanafil.
Sources that compare PDE5 inhibitor pharmacokinetics, duration, selectivity, or common adverse-effect patterns.
Class-level sources that describe shared PDE5/NO-cGMP mechanism.
Sources useful for deciding evidence-transfer rules rather than clinical benefit.
exclusion_criteria: Commercial comparison pages when official labels or indexed reviews were available.
Outcome-specific PDE5 studies that do not address agent transfer, pharmacokinetic comparability, selectivity, or class safety boundaries.
Non-U.S. PDE5 agents reserved for a later broader-class update.
Animal or in vitro studies not needed for the first agent-transfer framework unless summarized by comparative pharmacology reviews.
dedupe_method: Manual deduplication by label setid, PMID/DOI, source purpose, and whether the source adds a distinct transfer rule.

screening_decisions.title: DailyMed - CIALIS tadalafil tablet, film coated
screening_decisions.reason: Existing published source; needed for tadalafil half-life, PDE11/PDE6 selectivity, nitrate timing, daily exposure, and safety boundaries.

screening_decisions.title: DailyMed - VIAGRA sildenafil citrate tablet, film coated
screening_decisions.reason: Official sildenafil comparator label for half-life, nitrate contraindication, PDE6 selectivity, adverse reactions, and interaction boundaries.

screening_decisions.title: DailyMed - VARDENAFIL tablet, film coated
screening_decisions.reason: Current vardenafil comparator label for half-life, nitrate timing, PDE selectivity, QT warning, and interaction boundaries.

screening_decisions.title: DailyMed - STENDRA avanafil tablet
screening_decisions.reason: Official avanafil comparator label for short half-life, rapid dosing window, PDE selectivity, 12-hour nitrate boundary, and interaction warnings.

screening_decisions.title: Phosphodiesterase Type 5 (PDE5) Inhibitors - LiverTox overview
screening_decisions.reason: Accessible NCBI class overview for shared NO/cGMP/PDE5 mechanism and approved agent list.

screening_decisions.title: Phosphodiesterase type 5 inhibitor differentiation based on selectivity, pharmacokinetic, and efficacy profiles
screening_decisions.reason: Indexed comparative review directly differentiating sildenafil, tadalafil, and vardenafil by mechanism, pharmacokinetics, duration, food effects, and common adverse events.

screening_decisions.title: Potency, selectivity, and consequences of nonselectivity of PDE inhibition
screening_decisions.reason: Indexed comparative selectivity review used to define PDE6/PDE11 and other off-target transfer caveats.

screening_decisions.title: PDE5 inhibitors - pharmacology and clinical applications 20 years after sildenafil discovery
screening_decisions.reason: Useful modern pharmacology review, but not staged in this first framework because accessible labels plus NCBI overview and comparative reviews already support the transfer rules.

screening_decisions.title: Avanafil for the treatment of erectile dysfunction: initial data and clinical key properties
screening_decisions.reason: Potentially useful for avanafil details, but STENDRA label already provides the necessary avanafil comparator facts for this first framework.

screening_decisions.title: Commercial PDE5 inhibitor comparison pages
screening_decisions.reason: Not needed when official labels and indexed reviews are available.

screening_summary: Targeted searches found enough current label and indexed comparative pharmacology evidence to stage a project-level framework for PDE5 agent transfer. The framework keeps tadalafil-specific claims separate from adjacent sildenafil, vardenafil, avanafil, mixed PDE5, and class-level evidence.
limitations: Targeted baseline rather than systematic pharmacology review.
Comparative review extraction is abstract-level for Seftel 2004 and Bischoff 2004.
Non-U.S. PDE5 inhibitors and PAH-specific labels were not staged.
Outcome-specific transfer rules still need review-question-specific baselines.
class_transfer_limits: Search was designed to create project-level transfer rules. Included non-tadalafil evidence is class-level or adjacent comparator evidence, not direct tadalafil outcome evidence.
